# obs - Observability Subproject

`obs` is a library that simplifies open telemetry and logging in go. It provides a unified way to handle logging contexts and open telemetry spans.

## Key Features

It feels like `logr` and can be used to extract a logger from context:

```go
func Example(foo *Foo) {
    log := obs.FromContext(ctx)
    log.Info("hello world", "foo", foo)
    // ...
}
```

It supports creating spans that act as logging contexts:

```go
func Example(ctx context.Context, foo *Foo) {
    ctx, span := obs.Start(ctx, "roll")
    defer span.End()
    span.Info("hello world", "foo", foo)
}
```

Attributes can be added to the span easily. These attributes are added both to the open telemetry span and as values to the logger:

```go
func Example(ctx context.Context, foo *Foo) {
    ctx, span := obs.Start(ctx, "roll", obs.String("user.name", foo.UserName()))
    defer span.End()
    span.Info("hello world", "foo", foo)
}
```

You can also use custom strongly-typed helpers to create attributes:

```go
// Example helper:
// func Foo(f *Foo) obs.Attribute { return obs.String("foo", f.ID) }

func Example(ctx context.Context, foo *Foo) {
    ctx, log := obs.Start(ctx, "roll", Foo(foo))
    defer log.End()
    log.Info("hello world") // No reason to include foo here, it's already in the scope.
}
```
